case_metadata.court: Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals
case_metadata.project_type: construction improvements to lower Turkey Creek with underground piping under active railroad tracks

nodes.description: Appellant claimed stationary trains on railroad tracks constituted a Type II differing site condition that was unknown and unexpected, causing delays to boring and jacking operations.
nodes.source_excerpt: Appellant argued stationary trains were an unknown or unexpected condition allowing recovery as type 2 differing site condition

nodes.description: Appellant submitted RFI No. 0025 regarding boring operations when trains were parked on tracks, seeking clarification on work restrictions and potential compensation for delays.
nodes.source_excerpt: Appellant submitted RFI No. 0025 asking if boring was prohibited when stationary trains remained and if time/money adjustments would be provided

nodes.description: Government response to RFI No. 0025 imposed restrictions prohibiting pipe jacking when trains were stopped on tracks unless boring head had passed through loading zone.
nodes.source_excerpt: Government answered that pipe jacking shall not be permitted when train was stopped on track unless boring head had passed through loading zone

extraction_notes: The stationary trains condition is classified as Type II DSC based on appellant's claim, but the court has not made a final determination on its validity
The causal relationship between stationary trains and RFI submission is implied from the sequence of events described
